fields.summary: Replace RPC calls with WebService calls in LogsCLI

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

fields.description: Currently in LogsCLI, we still use YarnClient to get ContainerReport. We expect the users to enable yarn.timeline-service.generic-application-history.enabled to get finished container report which is not ideal. We can replace all RPC calls with WebService call, so the users do not need to change their configuration (to enable generic-application-history).

resolution.description: The problem described is an issue which will never be fixed.
resolution.name: Won't Fix

fields.summary: remove AHS enable check from LogsCLI#fetchAMContainerLogs

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

fields.summary: Use AHSClient in YarnClient when TimelineServer is running

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A problem which impairs or prevents the functions of the product.
issuetype.name: Bug

comments.body: To goal is to remove "yarn.timeline-service.generic-application-history.enabled" dependency. To use full functionality of Yarn Log CLI, we still need to enable the ATS. But we do not need to set "yarn.timeline-service.generic-application-history.enabled" as true.

bq. To goal is to remove "yarn.timeline-service.generic-application-history.enabled" dependency. 
Can we achieve this without completely replacing the RPC calls?

bq. Can we achieve this without completely replacing the RPC calls?

Yes, we can do that. We could close this one given YARN-5440 has been fixed.
